skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-ts
skill.description: Instrument applications with Azure Monitor and OpenTelemetry for JavaScript (@azure/monitor-opentelemetry). Use when adding distributed tracing, metrics, and logs to Node.js applications with Application Insights.
skill.summary: Instrument applications with Azure Monitor and OpenTelemetry for JavaScript (@azure/monitor-opentele...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, package names, and TypeScript method names. The skill is a documentation guide for Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry, but it legitimately instructs users to export telemetry and use environment-held connection strings, so publication should include a data and credential handling warning.

medium_findings.title: Telemetry Export Can Send Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: The skill shows how to configure Azure Monitor exporters and upload custom logs. This is expected for an observability skill, but application traces and logs can contain personal data, secrets, or regulated information if users do not filter them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly configure Azure Monitor exporters and upload logs to an Azure ingestion endpoint. The behavior is legitimate, but the data exposure risk is real for telemetry content.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Connection String Handling
medium_findings.description: The examples read an Application Insights connection string from an environment variable. This is the preferred pattern, but connection strings are sensitive and must not be logged, committed, or exposed in generated examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access is clearly present and relates to an Azure telemetry credential. It is not malicious, but it requires careful secret handling.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Execution False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at many locations. These are Markdown fenced examples for npm, node, TypeScript, and JSON, not instructions executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is inside Markdown code fences or inline documentation. No executable script file, tool hook, or command runner is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at the front matter description. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive text about Azure Monitor and OpenTelemetry. I found no cryptographic code or weak algorithm reference there.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance near span processor callback methods. The code shows OpenTelemetry interfaces and does not enumerate host state or collect system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines define onStart and onEnd methods for a span processor example. There is no command, file read, or host reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined code execution, network, and credential signals into a critical heuristic. Semantic review shows a documentation-only skill for Azure observability, with no hidden execution path or malicious endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious categories are present only as documentation examples for the stated telemetry use case. I found no prompt injection, obfuscation, or exfiltration domain.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Telemetry Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches users to send traces, metrics, and logs to Azure Monitor. Users should review telemetry fields before enabling collection in production.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network upload example is explicit and aligned with the skill purpose. Risk depends on the user's log content and Azure configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Based Telemetry Secret
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses an environment variable for the Application Insights connection string. This is appropriate, but the value should be protected as a secret.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential access is clear, but it uses a normal environment variable pattern rather than hardcoded secret material.

content.user_title: Instrument TypeScript Apps with Azure Monitor
content.value_statement: Teams need reliable traces, metrics, and logs for Node.js services. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry setup for TypeScript applications.
actual_capabilities: Shows installation commands for Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry packages.
Explains auto-instrumentation with useAzureMonitor for Node.js applications.
Provides examples for custom traces, metrics, logs, and span processors.
Covers ESM loader usage for Node.js 18.19 and later.
Demonstrates graceful shutdown and telemetry flushing patterns.
Lists key TypeScript imports for Azure Monitor exporters and ingestion clients.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not an executable instrumentation tool.
It does not validate Azure resources, keys, or connection strings.
It does not design privacy filters for each application domain.
Examples need adaptation for each framework, deployment, and compliance requirement.

use_cases.title: Add Observability to a Node.js API
use_cases.description: Use the skill to add Azure Monitor auto-instrumentation, traces, metrics, and shutdown handling to a TypeScript API.

use_cases.title: Standardize Application Insights Setup
use_cases.description: Use the skill to create consistent package, loader, and environment variable guidance across services.

use_cases.title: Tune Production Telemetry
use_cases.description: Use the skill to review sampling, custom dimensions, offline storage, and log ingestion options before release.

prompt_templates.title: Start Auto-Instrumentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation to this TypeScript Node.js app. Include packages, initialization order, and required environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Add Custom Telemetry
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add custom spans and metrics for this workflow using Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry. Keep sensitive values out of attributes and logs.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Exporters
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my manual OpenTelemetry setup and adapt it for Azure Monitor trace, metric, and log exporters in TypeScript.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry configuration for production readiness. Check sampling, offline storage, shutdown, privacy, and credential handling.

output_examples.input: Add Azure Monitor to an Express service.
output_examples.output: A setup plan with required packages, the earliest initialization point, the connection string environment variable, and shutdown handling.

output_examples.input: Improve telemetry for a slow checkout workflow.
output_examples.output: A tracing and metrics design that names spans, records useful attributes, and avoids storing payment or personal data.

output: Notes on log filtering and custom dimensions.
Checks for graceful shutdown and offline storage.

best_practices: Initialize Azure Monitor before importing instrumented application modules.
Store connection strings in secret-backed environment variables.
Filter logs and span attributes before sending telemetry to Azure.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode Application Insights connection strings in source files.
Do not export request bodies, tokens, or personal data as telemetry attributes.
Do not enable full sampling in high-traffic production services without cost review.

faq.question: Does this skill run telemetry code?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples for adding Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to TypeScript applications.

faq.question: Which package does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on @azure/monitor-opentelemetry, with related exporter and log ingestion packages.

faq.question: Can it help with ESM projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes the Node.js loader pattern for ESM applications on supported Node.js versions.

faq.question: Does it support custom metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes counter, histogram, and observable gauge examples using OpenTelemetry metrics.

faq.question: Is the connection string safe to share?
faq.answer: No. Treat the Application Insights connection string as sensitive configuration and store it securely.

faq.question: What should teams review before production use?
faq.answer: Review sampling, telemetry content, offline storage, graceful shutdown, and data retention requirements.
